(born 9 August 1986) is an Indian [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player from [Kozhikode, Kerala](/wiki/Kozhikode,_Kerala "Kozhikode, Kerala").[[1]](#cite_note-1) She was part of the national team that won the silver medal in [2010 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2010_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2010 Commonwealth Games"), also gold medals in 2004, 2006 and 2010 South Asian Games. She is 6 times National Champion in mixed doubles and 3 times National Champion in women's doubles. She represented India in many international badminton tournaments.

**Aparna Balan** (born 9 August 1986) is an Indian [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player from [Kozhikode, Kerala](/wiki/Kozhikode,_Kerala "Kozhikode, Kerala").[[1]](#cite_note-1) She was part of the national team that won the silver medal in [2010 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2010_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2010 Commonwealth Games"), also gold medals in 2004, 2006 and 2010 South Asian Games. She is 6 times National Champion in mixed doubles and 3 times National Champion in women's doubles. She represented India in many international badminton tournaments.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aparna_Balan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

In 2006, she won the national mixed doubles title partnering with [V. Diju](/wiki/V._Diju "V. Diju").[[2]](#cite_note-2) At the same year, she competed at the [2006 South Asian Games](/wiki/2006_South_Asian_Games "2006 South Asian Games") and won two silver medals in the women's and mixed doubles event.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In [2010 South Asian Games](/wiki/2010_South_Asian_Games "2010 South Asian Games"), Balan won the women's doubles gold with [Shruti Kurien](/wiki/Shruti_Kurien "Shruti Kurien") and mixed doubles silver with [Sanave Thomas](/wiki/Sanave_Thomas "Sanave Thomas").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aparna_Balan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

On 09 April 2018, she married Sandeep M S.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Achievements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aparna_Balan&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Achievements")]

### BWF Grand Prix

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aparna_Balan&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: BWF Grand Prix")]

The BWF Grand Prix had two levels, the [BWF Grand Prix and Grand Prix Gold](/wiki/BWF_Grand_Prix_and_Grand_Prix_Gold "BWF Grand Prix and Grand Prix Gold"). It was a series of badminton tournaments sanctioned by the [Badminton World Federation](/wiki/Badminton_World_Federation "Badminton World Federation") (BWF) which was held from 2007 to 2017.

*Women's doubles*

| [2009](/wiki/2009_Australian_Open_Grand_Prix#Finals_4 "2009 Australian Open Grand Prix") | [Australian Open](/wiki/Australian_Open_(badminton) "Australian Open (badminton)") | [India](/wiki/India "India") [Shruti Kurien](/wiki/Shruti_Kurien "Shruti Kurien") | [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") [Chia Chi Huang](/wiki/Chia_Chi_Huang "Chia Chi Huang")   [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") [Tang He Tian](/wiki/Tang_He_Tian "Tang He Tian") | 13–21, 19–21 | 2nd place, silver medalist(s) **Runner-up** |

### Major National Achievements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aparna_Balan&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Major National Achievements")]

* National champion in mixed doubles 2006
* National champion in mixed doubles 2007
* National champion in women doubles 2011
* National champion in mixed doubles 2012
* National champion in women doubles 2012
* National champion in mixed doubles 2013
* National champion in mixed doubles 2014
* National champion in mixed doubles 2015
* National champion in mixed doubles 2016
* National champion in women doubles 2017
* National games 2015 mixed doubles gold
* Premier Badminton League 2016 winners
